Date posted: May 1, 2026

Job description:

Lead with Purpose. Grow with Impact.

Reliable Medical Services, Inc. is looking for a dynamic and driven Administrator to lead our home health agency in Lombard, IL. This is an opportunity to take ownership of operations, build a strong team, and make a meaningful difference in the lives of patients.

If you’re a proactive leader who understands the complexities of home health and thrives in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment, we want to hear from you.

What You’ll Do:
As Administrator, you’ll be the driving force behind our agency’s success—balancing operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and patient-centered care.

Lead and manage the day-to-day operations of the home health agency
Ensure full compliance with federal, state, and local regulations, including Medicare and Medicaid guidelines
Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing team of clinical and administrative staff
Oversee hiring, onboarding, training, and performance management
Develop and implement policies, procedures, and best practices
Manage budgets, financial performance, and resource allocation to support growth
Maintain survey readiness and ensure accreditation standards are consistently met
Partner with clinical leadership to drive quality outcomes and exceptional patient care
Establish and strengthen relationships with referral sources and community partners
Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, patient satisfaction, and overall agency performance

What You Bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Administration, Nursing, Business, or related field (Master’s preferred)
At least 2 years of leadership experience in home health or a similar healthcare setting
Strong knowledge of home health regulations and compliance requirements
Proven ability to lead teams, manage operations, and drive results
Solid financial management and budgeting experience
Excellent communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills

Bonus Points If You Have:

Experience with a Medicare-certified home health agency
Familiarity with EMR systems

Why Join Reliable Medical Services, Inc.?

Opportunity to lead and shape a growing home health agency
Supportive leadership and collaborative team environment
Meaningful work that directly impacts patient lives
Competitive compensation and growth potential

Ready to Lead?
If you’re ready to take the next step in your leadership career and make a real impact, we encourage you to apply today.

Work Location: In person

Date posted: April 29, 2026

Job description:

Position Overview:
Reliable Medical Services, Inc. is seeking a detail-oriented and organized Intake Coordinator to manage the patient intake process for our home health agency. This role serves as a key point of contact for referrals, ensuring timely processing, accurate documentation, and a smooth transition for patients into care services.

Key Responsibilities:

Receive, review, and process patient referrals from hospitals, physicians, and other sources
Verify patient insurance coverage and eligibility
Coordinate with clinical staff to ensure appropriate patient admissions
Enter and maintain accurate patient information in the electronic medical record (EMR) system
Communicate with patients, families, and referral sources regarding admission status
Ensure all required documentation is completed and compliant with regulatory standards
Schedule initial patient visits and coordinate care start dates
Maintain strong relationships with referral partners and internal team members
Track and report intake metrics and referral outcomes

Qualifications:

High school diploma or equivalent required; Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree preferred
Minimum of 1 years of experience in healthcare intake, admissions, or administrative support (home health experience preferred)
Knowledge of insurance verification processes, including Medicare/Medicaid
Strong organizational and multitasking skills
Excellent communication and customer service abilities
Proficiency with EMR systems and Microsoft Office
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and meet deadlines

Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in a home health or hospice setting
Familiarity with medical terminology and coding basics

Join us as an Intake Coordinator and be part of a dedicated team committed to providing outstanding patient care. Your proactive energy and attention to detail will help create a seamless experience for every individual!

Benefits:

Opportunities for advancement

Work Location: In person

Date posted: March 31, 2026

Pay: From $45.00 per hour

Job description:

Lead with Purpose. Make a Difference Every Day.

At Reliable Medical Services, Inc., we believe exceptional care starts with exceptional leadership. We are looking for a passionate, driven, and experienced Director of Nursing (DON) who is ready to take the next step in their career and lead a dedicated home health team delivering high-quality, patient-centered care across the Chicagoland area.

If you’re a strong clinical leader who thrives in a collaborative environment and wants to shape the future of home health services, this is your opportunity to make a meaningful impact—both for patients and for the clinicians you lead.

Why Join Reliable Medical Services, Inc.?

Influence & Impact: Play a key leadership role in shaping clinical excellence and patient outcomes
Supportive Leadership Team: Work alongside professionals who value your expertise and vision
Growth Opportunity: Help expand and strengthen a growing home health agency
Work That Matters: Improve lives by ensuring patients receive compassionate care in the comfort of their homes

What You’ll Do

As Director of Nursing, you will serve as the clinical backbone of our organization, leading with integrity, expertise, and compassion.

Provide leadership and oversight for all clinical operations
Ensure full compliance with Illinois Department of Public Health (IDPH) regulations and Medicare Conditions of Participation
Mentor, develop, and inspire nursing staff to perform at their highest level
Drive quality improvement initiatives and ensure excellent patient outcomes
Oversee clinical documentation, audits, and regulatory readiness
Collaborate with physicians and interdisciplinary teams to coordinate seamless patient care
Develop and refine policies that support safe, effective, and compliant care delivery

What You Bring

Active Registered Nurse (RN) license in Illinois (required)
BSN required; Master’s degree preferred
2+ years of leadership experience in home health or a similar setting
Strong knowledge of Illinois home health regulations and Medicare/Medicaid standards
A proactive, solution-oriented leadership style
Excellent communication, organizational, and team-building skills
Passion for delivering high-quality, patient-centered care

Bonus Points If You Have

Previous Director of Nursing or Clinical Manager experience in home health
Experience with accreditation processes (Joint Commission, ACHC, etc.)
Background in quality assurance and performance improvement

What We Offer

Competitive salary (based on experience)
Career advancement opportunities
A leadership role where your voice truly matters

Ready to Lead? Apply Today.

If you’re ready to step into a leadership role where you can inspire a team, drive quality care, and make a real difference, we want to hear from you.

Apply now and become part of a team that’s redefining home health care in Illinois.

Work Location: In person
